title: Expressing support for the designation of the week of April 24-29, 2025, as "Small Businesses in For-Hire Transportation Week".
plain_summary: This bill wants to create a special week in April 2025 to honor small transportation businesses. These are small companies that help people get around by providing rides and transportation services. The bill recognizes that these businesses are important because they give jobs to many people and help transport millions of people every year. It also says these small transportation companies provided essential services during the COVID-19 pandemic when people really needed them.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Small Business.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution expresses support for naming the week of April twenty-fourth through twenty-ninth, two thousand twenty-five, as Small Businesses in For-Hire Transportation Week.

sections.title: Supporting Facts
sections.text: Small businesses in the for-hire transportation industry (companies that provide paid rides and transport services) include over eighteen thousand three hundred locally owned companies across the United States. These businesses employ two hundred seventy-six thousand two hundred fifty drivers and nearly one million other workers including mechanics, call center workers, office staff, and managers. About fifty percent of workers in this industry are minorities. These small transportation companies include medical transport services, taxi companies, limousine services, shuttle businesses, and paratransit services (transportation for people with disabilities). Together, they transport over two billion people every year in the United States. During COVID-19 and future health emergencies, these small businesses have been critical in helping families, hospital workers, patients, people who cannot leave their homes, working families, and people living in areas without grocery stores. Small transportation businesses continue to provide essential public transportation to people in communities who need it most. The Transportation Alliance is an important nonprofit organization that helps small business owners succeed by providing education, training, and mentoring. Congress should support using local transportation services because small businesses drive the economy in every community. A healthy for-hire transportation industry helps the local economy, creates more jobs, and keeps money in the community. Small businesses in the transportation industry continue to be an important resource for local, state, and federal government agencies by providing necessary transportation options for all citizens.

sections.title: Resolution Statement
sections.text: The House of Representatives expresses support for designating Small Businesses in For-Hire Transportation Week to honor the commitment and essential services provided by the for-hire transportation industry. The House also recognizes the efforts of small businesses in the for-hire transportation industry to promote safety and support their communities.
